raw_response: {"rating": 0.1, "lean": "Center", "explanation": "This is a news report presenting survey data from the Pew Research Center. The author's own prose consists almost entirely of neutral statistical reporting with no detectable ideological framing or loaded language. The piece presents data across partisan, racial, religious, and educational dimensions without favoring any side's position. Detailed Analysis: (1) The author's connecting prose is almost entirely numerical and descriptive — percentages, comparisons, and demographic breakdowns — with no evaluative or emotionally charged language applied to either pro-choice or pro-life positions; (2) Both Democratic and Republican views are reported with equal descriptive neutrality; neither side is characterized as correct, extreme, or unreasonable in the author's own voice; (3) The framing of the partisan gap as 'wider than it was two decades ago' is a factual observation supported by the data presented, not an ideological judgment; (4) Demographic breakdowns covering race, education, religion, and gender are presented symmetrically without privileging any group's perspective; (5) No sources beyond the survey instrument itself are cited, eliminating source-selection bias as a factor. The overall structure and language of the piece reflects standard social-science reporting with no meaningful directional lean."}

parsed_output.explanation: This is a news report presenting survey data from the Pew Research Center. The author's own prose consists almost entirely of neutral statistical reporting with no detectable ideological framing or loaded language. The piece presents data across partisan, racial, religious, and educational dimensions without favoring any side's position. Detailed Analysis: (1) The author's connecting prose is almost entirely numerical and descriptive — percentages, comparisons, and demographic breakdowns — with no evaluative or emotionally charged language applied to either pro-choice or pro-life positions; (2) Both Democratic and Republican views are reported with equal descriptive neutrality; neither side is characterized as correct, extreme, or unreasonable in the author's own voice; (3) The framing of the partisan gap as 'wider than it was two decades ago' is a factual observation supported by the data presented, not an ideological judgment; (4) Demographic breakdowns covering race, education, religion, and gender are presented symmetrically without privileging any group's perspective; (5) No sources beyond the survey instrument itself are cited, eliminating source-selection bias as a factor. The overall structure and language of the piece reflects standard social-science reporting with no meaningful directional lean.

explanation: This is a news report presenting survey data from the Pew Research Center. The author's own prose consists almost entirely of neutral statistical reporting with no detectable ideological framing or loaded language. The piece presents data across partisan, racial, religious, and educational dimensions without favoring any side's position. Detailed Analysis: (1) The author's connecting prose is almost entirely numerical and descriptive — percentages, comparisons, and demographic breakdowns — with no evaluative or emotionally charged language applied to either pro-choice or pro-life positions; (2) Both Democratic and Republican views are reported with equal descriptive neutrality; neither side is characterized as correct, extreme, or unreasonable in the author's own voice; (3) The framing of the partisan gap as 'wider than it was two decades ago' is a factual observation supported by the data presented, not an ideological judgment; (4) Demographic breakdowns covering race, education, religion, and gender are presented symmetrically without privileging any group's perspective; (5) No sources beyond the survey instrument itself are cited, eliminating source-selection bias as a factor. The overall structure and language of the piece reflects standard social-science reporting with no meaningful directional lean.
